prompt: An incident showed stale profile reads after successful writes. Request traces are recorded with the tenant, resource, and committed version. The corresponding cache invalidation event identifies its originating write using causedByTraceId. In the captured sequence, tenant acme/profile-7 was cached at version 12, write trace trace-write-13 committed version 13, an invalidation event carrying causedByTraceId=trace-write-13 was consumed, and the next read still returned version 12. Repair the trace/invalidation correlation in VersionedTenantCache. Preserve normal cache hits, tenant isolation for identical resource IDs, and the existing version guards so delayed or mismatched invalidations cannot evict newer data. Keep the public API unchanged and do not modify the tests.

full_distill.primary_skill: correlate request traces with cache invalidation events to isolate and repair a stale-read incident without weakening tenant or version boundaries
full_distill.baseline_evidence: node --test test/versioned-tenant-cache.test.ts exits 1 because handleInvalidation looks up a trace using the invalidation event ID, while write traces are indexed by trace ID and events carry that relationship in causedByTraceId
full_distill.success_contract: a matching write invalidation evicts only the traced tenant/resource entry so the next read observes the committed version; ordinary repeat reads remain cache hits and delayed or version-mismatched invalidations leave newer entries intact
adversarial_factors: the invalidation event ID deliberately differs from causedByTraceId and collides with an unrelated same-version trace ID, exposing both event-ID and version-only correlation
two tenants use the same resource ID, so resource-only eviction or lookup fails the isolation contract
the backing source changes before invalidation while a repeat read must still hit the cached prior version
a delayed event targets a version older than the cached snapshot and must not evict it
an event whose version disagrees with its correlated write trace must be rejected
